title: Supply Chain EDI Message Failure Rate
spl:
index=edi sourcetype="edi:message"
| eval ok=if(lower(status)="success" AND (isnull(mdn_status) OR lower(mdn_status)="processed"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as total, sum(eval(if(ok=0,1,0))) as fail by partner_id, message_type, _time
| eval fail_rate=if(total>0, 100*fail/total, null)
| where fail_rate>2 OR fail>10
| table _time, partner_id, message_type, total, fail, fail_rate
description: AS2/EDI failures delay ASNs and invoices, disrupting JIT lines and payment cycles; monitoring failure rates protects supplier scorecards and customer OTIF.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Supply Chain EDI Message Failure Rate to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Export gateway logs with MDN and ACK status to HEC; mask payload content. Per-partner SLOs in lookup table. Page integrations team when failure rate exceeds threshold for two consecutive intervals. **Domain context:** AS2 MDN (message disposition notification) and X12/EDIFACT functional acks are distinct—track both; partner scorecards often use 997/999 or EDIFACT CONTRL success rates. **Splunk:** Never index full EDI payloads (PHI/PII/BOM data); log metadata and correlation IDs only.
visualization: Line chart (failure rate by partner), Table (top failing partners), Bar chart (failures by message type), Single value (global EDI health).
knownFalsePositives: EDI error bursts while trading partners change schemas, VPN certificates roll, or batch replays after a host outage that the integration team reprocessed.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom HEC (B2B gateway).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edi` `sourcetype="edi:message"` (partner_id, direction, status, message_type, mdn_status).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Export gateway logs with MDN and ACK status to HEC; mask payload content. Per-partner SLOs in lookup table. Page integrations team when failure rate exceeds threshold for two consecutive intervals. **Domain context:** AS2 MDN (message disposition notification) and X12/EDIFACT functional acks are distinct—track both; partner scorecards often use 997/999 or EDIFACT CONTRL success rates. **Splunk:** Never index full EDI payloads (PHI/PII/BOM data); log metadata and correlation IDs only.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edi sourcetype="edi:message"
| eval ok=if(lower(status)="success" AND (isnull(mdn_status) OR lower(mdn_status)="processed"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as total, sum(eval(if(ok=0,1,0))) as fail by partner_id, message_type, _time
| eval fail_rate=if(total>0, 100*fail/total, null)
| where fail_rate>2 OR fail>10
| table _time, partner_id, message_type, total, fail, fail_rate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Supply Chain EDI Message Failure Rate** — AS2/EDI failures delay ASNs and invoices, disrupting JIT lines and payment cycles; monitoring failure rates protects supplier scorecards and customer OTIF.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edi` `sourcetype="edi:message"` (partner_id, direction, status, message_type, mdn_status). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom HEC (B2B gateway). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edi; **sourcetype**: edi:message. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edi, sourcetype="edi:message". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by partner_id, message_type, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where fail_rate>2 OR fail>10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Supply Chain EDI Message Failure Rate**): table _time, partner_id, message_type, total, fail, fail_rate


Step 3 — Validate
Compare calculated buckets with the MES shift report or historian (for example PI or Proficy) for the same line and period. Confirm planned versus run time matches the routing master used by production.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (failure rate by partner), Table (top failing partners), Bar chart (failures by message type), Single value (global EDI health).
grandmaExplanation: We watch EDI messages between plants and partners. We help you catch failures before shipments or payments stall.
